pattern: Reshaping a flat run of values into a fixed grid is just slicing it into equal chunks once the total count is confirmed to match rows times columns.
hints: Before building anything, check whether the total number of elements even matches the target grid size.
If the counts match, walk the flat array in fixed-size chunks; each chunk becomes one row of the result.
Return an empty list immediately when len(original) != m * n; otherwise slice indices [i*n, (i+1)*n) for each row i from 0 to m-1.

edge_inputs.description: element count does not match m*n, must return empty
requested grid larger than available elements
rectangular reshape with more rows than columns
rectangular reshape with more columns than rows

stress.description: large valid reshape sized from a random row/column split
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    total = max(1, size)
    cols = rng.randint(1, min(200, total))
    rows = total // cols
    if rows == 0:
        rows = 1
    total = rows * cols
    original = [rng.randint(1, 100000) for _ in range(total)]
    return [original, rows, cols]
stress.note: always produces a matching rows*cols so the reshape actually exercises the full array
